speaker: Daniel
quote_text: Compare our appearance
prompt_context: During the food test, Daniel asked that their appearance be compared with the youths who ate the royal food.
question: Who asked that his group’s appearance be compared after the ten-day food test?

speaker: Daniel
quote_text: Test your servants for ten days
prompt_context: While refusing the royal provisions, the Judean exile proposed a short trial with a simpler diet.
question: Who asked for a ten-day test with vegetables and water instead of the king's food?

speaker: Chief Official
quote_text: I fear my lord the king
prompt_context: When Daniel asked not to be defiled by the royal diet, the court official explained why he was afraid to grant the request.
question: Who told Daniel that he feared the king would notice a worse appearance and punish him?

speaker: Nebuchadnezzar
quote_text: You are trying to gain time
prompt_context: When the wise men asked again for the dream, the king accused them of trying to delay.
question: Who accused the wise men of trying to gain time when they could not reveal the dream?

speaker: Nebuchadnezzar
quote_text: My spirit is troubled
prompt_context: After a disturbing dream, the Babylonian king summoned his experts and demanded help.
question: Who said that his spirit was troubled to know the dream?

speaker: Wise Men
quote_text: Tell your servants the dream
prompt_context: The king's court experts first answered in the customary way, asking him to reveal what he had seen so they could interpret it.
question: Who told the king to tell them the dream so they could show its interpretation?

speaker: Nebuchadnezzar
quote_text: If you do not make known the dream
prompt_context: The king refused to tell his experts what he had dreamed and threatened severe punishment if they failed.
question: Who threatened to tear the wise men limb from limb if they could not reveal both the dream and its meaning?

speaker: Wise Men
quote_text: There is not a man on earth
prompt_context: Pressed to reveal the hidden dream itself, the king's experts answered that no human could do what he asked.
question: Who said that no one on earth could show the king's matter and that only the gods could reveal it?

speaker: Daniel
quote_text: Why is the decree so urgent?
prompt_context: When the order went out to kill the wise men, Daniel spoke carefully to the officer carrying it out.
question: Who asked Arioc why the king's decree was so urgent?

speaker: Daniel
quote_text: Do not destroy the wise men
prompt_context: After God revealed the mystery, Daniel went to Arioc and asked him not to destroy Babylon’s wise men.
question: Who told Arioc not to destroy the wise men of Babylon after the dream was revealed?

speaker: Daniel
quote_text: Blessed be the name of God
prompt_context: After the mystery of Nebuchadnezzar's dream was revealed to him in a night vision.
question: Who praised God after the mystery of Nebuchadnezzar's dream was revealed?

speaker: Daniel
quote_text: No wise men can show the king the mystery
prompt_context: Brought before Nebuchadnezzar, Daniel first denied that human wisdom could answer the king's demand and pointed to heaven instead.
question: Who told Nebuchadnezzar that no wise men could reveal the mystery, but that there is a God in heaven who reveals mysteries?

speaker: Daniel
quote_text: You saw, O king
prompt_context: As Daniel began recounting the dream itself, he described the enormous image seen by the king.
question: Who began the interpretation by saying, "You saw, O king," and describing the great image?

speaker: Daniel
quote_text: You are the head of gold
prompt_context: Interpreting the dream, Daniel identified the first kingdom represented in the image.
question: Who told Nebuchadnezzar, "You are the head of gold"?

speaker: Nebuchadnezzar
quote_text: Truly, your God is God of gods
prompt_context: After hearing the dream and its interpretation, the Babylonian king responded with awe to Daniel.
question: Who said to Daniel that his God was God of gods and Lord of kings?

speaker: Nebuchadnezzar
quote_text: Is it true?
prompt_context: When Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego were accused of refusing to worship the image, the king questioned them directly and offered one last chance.
question: Who asked Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego whether it was true that they would not serve his gods?

speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego
quote_text: We have no need to answer you in this matter
prompt_context: Standing before the furnace, the three Judean officials answered the king with fearless loyalty to God.
question: Who told Nebuchadnezzar that they had no need to answer him in that matter and would not serve his gods?

speaker: Nebuchadnezzar
quote_text: Did we not cast three men bound into the fire?
prompt_context: After the men were thrown into the furnace, the king was astonished at what he saw inside.
question: Who asked whether three bound men had been thrown into the fire and then said he saw four men walking free?

speaker: Nebuchadnezzar
quote_text: Servants of the Most High God, come out
prompt_context: Seeing the three unharmed in the furnace, the king called them to come out.
question: Who addressed Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego as servants of the Most High God and told them to come out?

speaker: Nebuchadnezzar
quote_text: Blessed be the God of Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego
prompt_context: After the three men emerged unharmed, the king publicly praised their God and made a decree.
question: Who blessed the God of Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego after the furnace miracle?

speaker: Nebuchadnezzar
quote_text: I saw a dream that made me afraid
prompt_context: In his proclamation, the king described a later dream that deeply disturbed him while he was at ease in his house.
question: Who said that he saw a dream that made him afraid while resting in his house?

speaker: Nebuchadnezzar
quote_text: You are able, for the spirit of the holy gods is in you
prompt_context: After his advisers failed again, the king turned to Daniel with confidence that he could interpret the troubling tree dream.
question: Who told Daniel that he was able to interpret the dream because a holy spirit was in him?

speaker: Daniel
quote_text: My lord, may the dream be for those who hate you
prompt_context: Before explaining the meaning of the great tree, Daniel showed distress over what it meant for the king.
question: Who wished that Nebuchadnezzar's dream might apply to his enemies rather than to him?

speaker: Daniel
quote_text: Break off your sins by practicing righteousness
prompt_context: After interpreting the dream, Daniel urged the king to respond with repentance and mercy.
question: Who counseled Nebuchadnezzar to break off his sins by righteousness and show mercy to the oppressed?

speaker: Voice from Heaven
quote_text: The kingdom has departed from you
prompt_context: While the proud king was still boasting over Babylon, judgment was announced from heaven.
question: Who declared to Nebuchadnezzar that the kingdom had departed from him and that he would live like the beasts?

speaker: Nebuchadnezzar
quote_text: I blessed the Most High
prompt_context: After his reason returned, the humbled king publicly honored the God who rules forever.
question: Who said that he blessed the Most High after his reason returned to him?

speaker: Belshazzar
quote_text: Whoever reads this writing
prompt_context: When the mysterious hand wrote on the wall during the feast, the terrified king offered rewards for an interpretation.
question: Who promised purple clothing, a gold chain, and third place in the kingdom to whoever read the writing?

speaker: Queen Mother
quote_text: There is a man in your kingdom
prompt_context: When Belshazzar and his lords were helpless before the writing, the queen mother reminded him of a proven interpreter from earlier days.
question: Who told Belshazzar that there was a man in his kingdom in whom was the spirit of the holy gods?

speaker: Belshazzar
quote_text: Are you that Daniel?
prompt_context: Once Daniel was brought in, the king tested whether the stories about him were true and repeated the reward offer.
question: Who asked Daniel whether he was one of the exiles from Judah and said he had heard that he could interpret enigmas?

speaker: Daniel
quote_text: Let your gifts be for yourself
prompt_context: Before interpreting the writing, Daniel refused the king's rewards while agreeing to explain the message.
question: Who told Belshazzar to keep his gifts and give his rewards to another?

speaker: Daniel
quote_text: You have not humbled your heart
prompt_context: In rebuking the king at the feast, Daniel contrasted Belshazzar's pride with what he should have learned from Nebuchadnezzar.
question: Who told Belshazzar that he had not humbled his heart though he knew all this?

speaker: Daniel
quote_text: Mene, Mene, Tekel, and Parsin
prompt_context: Daniel gave the meaning of each word written by the mysterious hand on the palace wall.
question: Who interpreted the writing on the wall for Belshazzar?

speaker: Officials
quote_text: All the presidents of the kingdom have agreed
prompt_context: Jealous administrators came to Darius with a proposal designed to trap Daniel through his faithfulness in prayer.
question: Who told Darius that all the officials had agreed he should establish an irrevocable injunction?

speaker: Officials
quote_text: Daniel pays no attention to you
prompt_context: After the decree was signed, the conspirators returned to Darius with an accusation about Daniel's prayers.
question: Who accused Daniel before Darius of paying no attention to the king's injunction?

speaker: Darius
quote_text: May your God deliver you
prompt_context: Unable to overturn his own decree, the king spoke hopefully to Daniel as he was cast into the den.
question: Who told Daniel that his God, whom he served continually, would deliver him?

speaker: Darius
quote_text: O Daniel, servant of the living God
prompt_context: At daybreak after a sleepless night, the king hurried to the den and called out anxiously.
question: Who cried out to Daniel at the lion's den asking whether the living God had delivered him?

speaker: Daniel
quote_text: O king, live forever
prompt_context: From inside the den, Daniel answered the king with news of divine protection and his own innocence.
question: Who told Darius that God had sent his angel and shut the lions' mouths?

speaker: Darius
quote_text: I make a decree
prompt_context: After Daniel's rescue, the king sent a proclamation throughout his realm honoring Daniel's God.
question: Who decreed that people throughout his kingdom should tremble before the God of Daniel?

speaker: Daniel
quote_text: My spirit was anxious
prompt_context: After seeing the four beasts in a night vision, Daniel admitted his distress before seeking understanding.
question: Who said that his spirit was anxious within him because of the vision?

speaker: Daniel
quote_text: I desired to know the truth
prompt_context: After seeing the beasts, Daniel wanted more understanding about the fourth beast in the vision.
question: Who desired to know the truth about the fourth beast in the vision?

speaker: Voice from the Ulai
quote_text: These four great beasts are four kings
prompt_context: In response to Daniel's question, a heavenly interpreter explained the broad meaning of the vision.
question: Who told Daniel that the four great beasts were four kings and that the saints would receive the kingdom?

speaker: Gabriel
quote_text: Gabriel, make this man understand the vision
prompt_context: During Daniel's vision by the Ulai, a commanding voice instructed the heavenly interpreter to explain what Daniel had seen.
question: Who told Gabriel to make Daniel understand the vision?

speaker: Gabriel
quote_text: I will make known to you what shall be at the latter end
prompt_context: As Gabriel explained the ram and the goat, he told Daniel the vision concerned the appointed time of the end.
question: Who told Daniel that he would make known what would happen at the latter end of the indignation?

speaker: Daniel
quote_text: O Lord, the great and awesome God
prompt_context: While seeking mercy for Jerusalem from Jeremiah's prophecy, Daniel confessed the sins of his people in prayer.
question: Who began praying, "O Lord, the great and awesome God," while confessing Israel's sins?

speaker: Daniel
quote_text: O Lord, hear
prompt_context: Near the end of his prayer, Daniel pleaded urgently for forgiveness, attention, and action for the sake of God's name.
question: Who prayed, "O Lord, hear; O Lord, forgive; O Lord, pay attention and act"?

speaker: Angel
quote_text: I have now come out to give you insight
prompt_context: While Daniel was still praying, the heavenly messenger came swiftly with an answer about understanding and the beloved prophet.
question: Who told Daniel that he had come to give him insight and understanding?

speaker: Angel
quote_text: O Daniel, man greatly loved
prompt_context: After Daniel's period of mourning, a heavenly messenger strengthened him and urged him to stand upright.
question: Who addressed Daniel as a man greatly loved and told him to stand upright?

speaker: Angel
quote_text: Now I will show you the truth
prompt_context: The heavenly messenger continued by unveiling future conflicts involving Persia, Greece, and later kingdoms.
question: Who told Daniel, "Now I will show you the truth," before describing future kings?

speaker: Daniel
quote_text: O my lord, what shall be the outcome?
prompt_context: After hearing the vision of the end, Daniel admitted that he did not understand and asked what the result would be.
question: Who asked, "O my lord, what shall be the outcome of these things?"

speaker: Man Clothed in Linen
quote_text: Go your way, Daniel
prompt_context: In the closing exchange of the book, the heavenly figure answered Daniel's question about the end.
question: Who told Daniel to go his way because the words were shut up and sealed until the time of the end?
